english: pf. _opam ('pham), opp. to rgyál-ba (rgyal ba) to be beaten, conquered, to come off a loser, to get the worst of, yyúl(-las) (g.yul (-las)) in battle Dzl.; lha-ma-yín-las (lha ma yin las) by the Asuras Dom.; in law-suits, in traffic etc.; _opám-par ('pham par) gyúr-ba (gyur ba) B., *pam (pham) dó-wa* (do wa) C. id; also with _opam ('pham), as if it were a sbst.: *pam (pham) kúr-wa* (kur wa) C. to put up with, to bear a loss, damage, defeat; _opam ('pham) bláñ-ba (blang ba) Glr., Pth. prob. id.; _opám-par ('pham par) byéd-pa (byed pa) to beat, to defeat, to conquer, rgyá-rnams (rgya rnams) _opám-par ('pham par) byas (byas) he conquered the Chinese Glr.; rás-pas (ras pas) bón-po (bon po) čós-kyis (chos kyis) _opám-byas-te ('pham byas te) Raspa overcoming the Bonpo by the doctrine of Buddha (v. čos (chos) 3.) Mil.; *pam (pham) čúg-če (cug ce) or kál-če* (khal ce), W. id.; _opam ('pham) _opog ('phog) soñ (song) I have met with a loss, I suffered damage, opp. to gyal (rgyal) tob (thob) soñ (song); _opam-rgyál ('pham rgyal) ma (ma) bsrés-na (bsres na) if one is not inclined now for a serious struggle, will not stand the chance of... Mil.; yid-_opám-pa (yid 'pham pa) Mil., *sems (sems) pám-po* (pham po) C. dejection; yid-_opám-ma (yid 'pham ma) a low-spirited, dejected woman Mil.: _opám-pa ('pham pa) Glr., _opám-po ('pham po) the vanquished etc.; *pam-pa (pham pa) ño-lén (ngo len) čó-če* (co ce) W. to give in, to ask pardon; mí-_opám (mi 'pham) 1. invincible. 2. a man's name. 3. mi-_opam (mi 'pham) myón-po (myon po) Zam., also mi-_opám (mi 'pham) čós-kyi (chos kyi) rje (rje) is stated to be = _oJam-dbyáñs ('jam dbyangs).
Cs. in compounds: board, sgo-_opar (sgo 'phar) board or leaf of a door.

I. sbst. v, púr-ba (phur ba). II. vb. (vb. n. to spór-ba (spor ba)) 1. to rebound, of stones, *bar-náñ-la* (bar nang la) W. to splash up, of water, to fly up, of sparks; to leap, to bound, to throb, of the veins, rtsa (rtsa) _opar ('phar), the pulse is beating; *par (phar) tá-če* (tha ce) W., to feel one's pulse; *nyíñ-ka (nying ka) par (phar) dug* (dug) his heart is throbbing, palpitating; *pár-ra (phar ra) rag* (rag) I have heart-throbbing (v. rag (rag)); _opár-_opro ('phar 'phro) čad (chad) v. _opró-ba ('phro ba) 2; sá-la (sa la) _opár-ba ('phar ba), to fidget, to be restless, to jump, from fear Pth.; pár-gyis (phar gyis) _opár-ba ('phar ba) Lex. prob. the same as _opár-ba ('phar ba). - 2. Cs. to be raised, elevated, promoted, advanced.
Sch. 'double, manifold'; brgya-_opar-ma (brgya 'phar ma), Sch. 'more than hundred'.

pf. _opuñ ('phung), to sink, to begin to decay, to be in declining circumstances, to get into misery, either by one's own fault, or that of others (opp. to tséñ-ba (tsheng ba)) Glr. and elsewh.; bód-yul (bod yul) _opúñ-bai ('phung bai) las (las) a deed to the detriment of Tibet Glr.; in a similar manner bód-yul (bod yul) _opúñ-bai ('phung bai) _opúñ-góñ ('phung gong), mischievous conjurers in order to inflict an injury on Tibet Ld.-Glr. Schl. 21, b; mgár-gyis (mgar gyis) rgyá-yul (rgya yul) _opúñ-bar ('phung bar) byás-pa-rnams (byas pa rnams) drán-nas (dran nas), remembering the calamities brought on China by Mgar Glr.; _opúñ-bar ('phung bar) gyúr-ba (gyur ba) B., *_opúñ-du ('phung du) dó-wa* (do wa) C., *_opuñ ('phung) čá-če* (ca ce) W., to be ruined, to perish, _opuñ-bar ('phung bar) byed-pa (byed pa) B., _opúñ-la ('phung la) sbyór-ba (sbyor ba) Mil., prob. also ytóñ-ba (gtong ba), _ojúg-pa ('jug pa) to ruin, to undo Pth.; rañ-_opúñ (rang 'phung) having been reduced by one's own fault; _opúñ-dkrol ('phung dkrol) or krol (khrol) the decay of fortune, ruin, destruction Mil. and elsewh.; _opúñ-yži ('phung gzhi) cause, occasion of decay Mil.
to lay aside, to put away, to separate, = _obúd-pa ('bud pa) Cs. (?), súg-pa (sug pa) _opúd-pa ('phud pa), to clear, to part the flour from the bran, to sieve Sch. (?)
= _obúb-pa ('bub pa) to cover with a roof Sch. (?)

pf. _opur ('phur), 1. to fly; púr-gyis (phur gyis) _opúr-ba ('phur ba) Lex., prob. id.; cf. par-ba (phar ba). - 2. to wrap up, envelop, muffle up; Dzl. ༢༧༨, 10: rin-po-če (rin po che) gós-kyi (gos kyi) mtá-mar (mtha' mar) the gem into the skirt of the coat, and likewise Dzl. ༢༧༦, 13 read: gós-mtar (gos mthar) púr-te (phur te), inst. of byúñ-ste (byung ste); mgo (mgo) gós-kyis (gos kyis) Mil. (col. not used). - 3. = mnyéd-pa (mnyed pa) to rub with the hand, e.g. linen in washing, leather in tanning Glr.; to scratch (softly) C.

prefix, de sogs (de sogs) da-yig (da yig) gáb-_opul-čan (gab 'phul can), these and others have d with the prefix g: bás-_opul-kao (bas 'phul kao) words beginning with k with the prefix b; bá-yis (ba yis) _opúl-bai ('phul bai) sla (sla), viz. bsla (bsla)...; das-_opúl-méd (das 'phul med) these receive no d as prefix; sa- (sa)
